chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with utoni/ptunnel-ng (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with tnich/ligolo-ng (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with carlospolop/PEASS-ng (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the osint category. Fits when the engagement needs: Recon-ng content now available on Pluralsight!.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Recon-ng content now available on Pluralsight!.
documentation.how_to_use: Clone or install `recon-ng` from its upstream repository, then run `recon-ng --help` (or the README entrypoint) to confirm flags. Recon-ng content now available on Pluralsight!.

name: recon-ng
summary: Recon-ng content now available on Pluralsight!

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need osint, use recon-ng because Recon-ng content now available on Pluralsight!.
When chaining osint tools, pair recon-ng with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `recon-ng --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
